title: National Information Infrastructure
content: The National Information Infrastructure (NII) was the product of the High Performance Computing Act of 1991. It was a telecommunication s policy buzzword, which was popularized during the Clinton Administration under the leadership of Vice-President Al Gore. It proposed to build communications networks, interactive services, interoperable computer hardware and software, computers, databases, and consumer electronics in order to put vast amounts of information available to both public and private sectors.
